source: Learning Rsync - rsync demo 02: Pull/push against a daemon module and fixing permissions

questions.q: Which syntax targets an rsync-daemon module (note the punctuation)?
questions.why: A double colon :: (or the rsync:// URL) addresses a daemon module; a single colon : means SSH.

questions.q: Which is the pull/download form from the daemon share to /root?
questions.why: The source is the daemon module (host::share), the destination local.

questions.q: A push fails with 'Connection reset by peer'. Which server setting caused it?
options: read only = yes on the module (set it to no and restart)
questions.why: The module was read-only; change read only = no and restart rsyncd.

questions.q: After enabling writes, a push still fails 'Permission denied'. Why?
options: The virtual user maps to the system user nobody, which lacks write on the directory
questions.why: By default the virtual user li maps to nobody, which has no write permission on /rsync/.

questions.q: How is that write-permission problem fixed?
options: setfacl -m u:nobody:rwx /rsync/  (grant nobody rwx via an ACL)
questions.why: An ACL granting nobody rwx on the directory lets the mapped user write.

questions.q: After editing rsyncd.conf, what must you do for the change to take effect?
options: Restart the daemon: systemctl restart rsyncd.service
questions.why: Config changes require a daemon restart.
